provenance.description: Completeness candidates discovered by panel gap-analysis — additional defensible correspondences MISSING from the operational mapping. Logged for human review; NOT auto-applied.
provenance.note: UNREFUTED completeness proposals — NOT applied without further human review

candidates.rationale: BSI-C5 COM-04 — The existing COM-04->OIS-04 entry itself states COM-04's compliance-review and reporting-function parts are NOT covered by SoD (OIS-04) and instead live in EUCS A16 'Compliance and Information Security Reviews'; it even carries cross_refs:['A16-1'] but no actual mapping row. The inverse file confirms A16-1->COM-04 as partial_overlap. COM-04 is the Beauftragten-/Berichtswesen-Funktion (compliance steering + reporting + internal review), which is precisely the A16 review-program scope. This is a strongly defensible, currently-missing partial_overlap correspondence.

BSI-C5 COM-04 — The inverse mapping A18-1 (Investigative Requests from Government Agencies) explicitly cross-refs COM-04 ('regulatorische Steuerung' fragments existed under COM-04 before C5:2026 INQ existed). Since COM-04 carried the fragmentary regulatory/authority-handling content, a low-confidence partial_overlap COM-04->A18-1 is defensible and currently absent. Kept low because C5:2020 had no dedicated authority-request criterion; the bulk of A18 has no C5:2020 home (EUCS superset).

BSI-C5 SSO-01 — The inverse mapping A18-1 (Investigative Requests from Government Agencies) explicitly cross-refs SSO-01, because C5:2020 captured authority-request handling only fragmentarily inside sub-provider/third-party contract clauses governed by SSO-01 ('Richtlinien fuer Beziehungen zu Dritten'). A low-confidence partial_overlap SSO-01->A18-1 is therefore defensible and currently missing. Kept low: SSO-01 is primarily a third-party-relationship policy, and A18 is largely a EUCS superset with no real C5:2020 counterpart.

BSI-C5 IDM-09 (Zugriffskontrolle / enforcement of access control to systems and data) is currently mapped only to EUCS IAM-08 (access enforcement / restriction). IDM-09's scope of granting and enforcing access to information explicitly covers the provisioning/access-to-data dimension that EUCS isolates in IAM-03 (Access to information / management of access rights to data). This is a genuinely separable correspondence: IAM-08 governs the technical enforcement mechanism, IAM-03 governs the data-level access decision. Defensible additional mapping; not full because IDM-09's primary center of gravity remains the IAM-08 enforcement control.

BSI-C5 OPS-06: OPS-06 'Datensicherung' is a backup-management control and belongs squarely in the EUCS Operational Security (OPS) data-backup objective, which requires backup concepts, scope, frequency and protection of backup data. The current sole mapping to 'A11-1' does not cover the EUCS OPS backup control, which is the direct functional equivalent and is missing.

BSI-C5 OPS-06: OPS-06 explicitly requires regular restoration tests of backups to verify recoverability, which is the same objective as the EUCS Business Continuity recoverability/restore-test control. This restore-test correspondence is a genuine cross-category link to EUCS BC and is missing from the current mapping.

candidates.target: EUCS OIS-04 (Contact with Authorities and Special Interest Groups)
candidates.rationale: BSI-C5 OIS-05: OIS-05 'Kontakt zu Behoerden' (contact with authorities) corresponds directly to the EUCS Organisation of Information Security control on maintaining contact with relevant authorities/special interest groups. The current mapping to RM-02 (risk management) is in the wrong category; the OIS authority-contact control is the defensible correspondence and is missing.

candidates.target: EUCS PM-01 (Policy for Security in Supplier/Procurement Relationships - Security Requirements)
candidates.rationale: BSI-C5 DEV-01: DEV-01 covers defining security requirements at the start of acquisition/development; where development or system components are procured/outsourced, these security requirements are also addressed by the EUCS Procurement Management objective on contractually agreed supplier security requirements. This acquisition-side correspondence to EUCS PM is defensible and missing, while the in-house development equivalent (DEV-01) is already mapped.
